skill.name: security-requirement-extraction
skill.description: Derive security requirements from threat models and business context. Use when translating threats into actionable requirements, creating security user stories, or building security test cases.
skill.summary: Transform threat analysis into actionable security requirements with templates for user stories, compliance mapping, and test case generation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate security education and requirements management tool. All 39 static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code fences, hardcoded URLs are documentation references, and security domain enum values were incorrectly flagged. The skill provides Python templates for extracting security requirements from threat models.

high_findings.title: External Commands False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at multiple locations in implementation-playbook.md and SKILL.md. These are backticks in markdown code fences for Python code samples, NOT actual shell execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks are markdown formatting for code blocks, not shell execution operators. The skill contains Python code samples, not actual shell commands.

high_findings.title: Network Access False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged hardcoded URLs at lines 674-676. These are legitimate documentation links to OWASP ASVS, NIST SP 800-53, and a book reference.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static documentation references, not network calls made by the skill. The skill does not make any network requests.

high_findings.title: Sensitive Data False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Windows SAM database' at lines 78 and 444. These are enum values for CRYPTOGRAPHY security domain, not actual SAM database access.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Scanner incorrectly triggered on the word 'cryptography'. There is no Windows SAM database access in this code.

high_findings.title: C2 Keywords False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'C2 keywords' at line 86. This is OWASP enum value, not command-and-control.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The string 'owasp' is the Open Web Application Security Project, a legitimate security framework, not a C2 indicator.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple lines. These are references to cryptographic concepts as security domains, not actual weak crypto implementations.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Scanner triggered on crypto-related terms in the code. No weak cryptographic algorithms are recommended or used.

high_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged system reconnaissance at multiple lines. These are method names for threat-to-requirement extraction, not security reconnaissance.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Methods like get_by_threat() and _threat_to_requirements() are for security requirement extraction, not system reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Extract Security Requirements from Threat Models
content.value_statement: Transform threat analysis into actionable security requirements. This skill provides templates and patterns for creating security user stories, compliance mappings, and test specifications from threat models.
actual_capabilities: Convert threat models to structured security requirements
Generate security-focused user stories with acceptance criteria
Map security requirements to compliance frameworks (PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, SOC2, NIST, ISO 27001, OWASP)
Create test specifications from security requirements
Build traceability matrices between threats and requirements
Identify compliance gaps in security requirements
limitations: Does not perform actual threat modeling - requires threat model as input
Does not execute or validate security tests
Does not provide legal compliance certification
Templates require customization for specific project contexts

use_cases.title: Security Engineer Creating Requirements
use_cases.description: A security engineer has completed a STRIDE threat model and needs to transform identified threats into actionable security requirements with testable acceptance criteria.

use_cases.title: Developer Writing Security Stories
use_cases.description: A developer needs to write security-focused user stories that integrate with agile workflows and include proper acceptance criteria and test cases.

use_cases.title: Compliance Officer Mapping Controls
use_cases.description: A compliance officer needs to map security requirements to multiple regulatory frameworks and identify gaps in coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Requirement Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-requirement-extraction skill to convert this threat model into security requirements. The threat is: [describe threat with category, target, impact, likelihood]. Generate requirements with traceability, acceptance criteria, and test cases.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extracting requirements from a single threat

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Framework Compliance Mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the security-requirement-extraction skill, map these security requirements to PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and GDPR frameworks. Identify which controls are covered and what gaps exist. Requirements: [list requirements]
prompt_templates.scenario: Mapping requirements to multiple compliance frameworks

prompt_templates.title: Security User Story Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate security user stories from these requirements using the security-requirement-extraction skill. Include the standard story format with acceptance criteria and definition of done. Requirements: [list requirements]
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating security user stories for agile

prompt_templates.title: Full Security Requirements Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the security-requirement-extraction skill, generate a complete security requirements package from this threat model: [threat model details]. Include requirement models with all attributes, threat-to-requirement mappings, compliance matrix, and gap analysis for [specify framework].

output_examples.input: Extract security requirements for: Threat: T001, Category: INFORMATION_DISCLOSURE, Target: User database, Impact: HIGH, Likelihood: MEDIUM
output_examples.output: Generated 3 security requirements with STRIDE mapping, priority HIGH, acceptance criteria, and test cases. Mapped to GDPR Article 32 and OWASP V8.1 controls.

output_examples.input: Map requirements SR-001 through SR-005 to PCI-DSS controls
output_examples.output: Compliance matrix showing coverage: Authentication (8.1, 8.2, 8.3), Data Protection (3.4, 3.5), Audit Logging (10.1, 10.2). No gaps identified.

best_practices: Start with a complete threat model before extracting requirements
Ensure each requirement links back to at least one threat or compliance control
Include testable acceptance criteria for every requirement
Review generated requirements with security and development teams
anti_patterns: Copying generic security requirements without threat traceability
Creating requirements without acceptance criteria or test cases
Skipping compliance mapping until late in development
Using requirements as replace for actual security testing

faq.question: What threat modeling frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill uses STRIDE (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege) as the primary threat categorization, but the requirement templates work with any threat model format.

faq.question: Can this skill generate requirements for cloud security?
faq.answer: Yes, the requirement templates focus on security domains (authentication, authorization, data protection, etc.) that apply to cloud, on-premises, and hybrid environments. You may need to add cloud-specific controls as custom requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with specific security tools?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates text-based requirements, user stories, and compliance mappings. The output can be imported into requirements management tools, JIRA, or documentation systems manually.

faq.question: How do I validate the generated requirements?
faq.answer: Review each requirement against the source threat model, verify acceptance criteria are testable, and confirm compliance mappings are accurate for your specific regulatory context.

faq.question: What compliance frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: The skill includes mappings for PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, SOC2, NIST CSF, ISO 27001, and OWASP ASVS. You can extend these with additional frameworks as needed.

faq.question: Can this skill help with security acceptance testing?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill generates test cases for each requirement. These test cases can be used to create automated security tests or manual test plans.
